input.tenant_message: Water pressure in my apartment has been really weak for about two weeks now. It's bad at every faucet — kitchen, both bathrooms, even the shower feels like a trickle. My neighbor in 3A mentioned their pressure seems fine so I think it might be something specific to my unit. I checked and the shutoff valves under the sinks look fully open. Attaching a video but it just saved as a photo so hopefully you can see the flow rate.

ai_output.recommended_action: Schedule a plumbing inspection within the week to check the unit's pressure-reducing valve (PRV) and supply lines for partial blockage or sediment buildup — unit-wide low pressure isolated to one apartment suggests a PRV failure or clogged aerators throughout. The plumber should test pressure at the main shutoff and compare to adjacent units.
